Patch Set 4:

Niu Yawei, why a negative inspection is set? if you do not understand the race, it does not mean it does not exist. a negative inspection is supposed to be set if you see defects in the patch. please point to the found defects or remove your '-1'.

Patch Set 4:

My intention is not to write on  disk that client was evicted if failover was started. 
I have a reproducer but we agreed not to land it because it is rather heavy.

Patch Set 4:

Niu is quite correct to give a -1 if there is something that is unclear. When the  comments are elaborated so that he does understand the intention then he will of course remove it

Patch Set 4:

ok, it is Intel business, but my IMHO it is not correct. the semantic of '-1' is the patch is to be re-worked because it has problems, whereas 'unclear' does not mean that. i.e. +1/-1 is set when an inspector has an opinion the patch is good/bad and 0 means it is _unclear_, and still under review.

Patch Set 4:

There are two problems:
1. I don't see any essential difference between checking the export flag (OBD_OPT_FAILOVER) or obd_fail.
2. Assume checking obd_fail can fix the race, then the export flag should be removed, since it's useless anymore.

That's why I gave -1 here.

Patch Set 4:

I realized that obd_fail is set at init time by default (but not on umount), so checking obd_fail can reduce race rather thank checking the flags (which is set during umount time).

So the OBD_OPT_FAILOVER flag should be removed since it's useless now.
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 4:

I've removed OBD_OPT_FAILOVER as it isn't used any more.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

Please see my comment in commit message.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Niu Yawei, I added an explanation about the test case and the race we saw. see a reply to you question.

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

The main intention of the patch is to disable any modification on disk when failover was started already. 

And now both ptl_send_rpc() and tgt_client_del() check save variable obd_fail, so it won't race any more.

Patch Set 5:

I'm even confused, I was thinking the intention of the patch is to avoid delete the client slot in last_rcvd when failover. tgt_client_del() checks obd_fail on a target device, ptl_send_rpc() checks obd_fail on a client device, why are they correlated?

Patch Set 5:

A server may wants to send BL AST when failover has started, so it gets an error and evicts the client.

Patch Set 5:

For the case you mentioned ("sending ast failed because of device is in failover umount, then client is evicted"), could you explain how it correlates with class_disconnect_export_list() and the OBD_OPT_FAILOVER flag?

From what I can see, it goes class_fail_export() code path, and the OBD_OPT_FAILOVER is never been set for such case, did I miss anything?

Patch Set 5:

On evict OBD_OPT_FAILOVER is checked by tgt_client_del() to decide if ckient changes should be written to disk.

Patch Set 5:

I'm not sure if you agree with me on the root cause conclusion: OBD_OPT_FAILOVER flag isn't set in that case. (it's not a race, but we missed adding the flag in that case).

If we are on the same page about the root cause, then we can go further to discuss how to fix it.

My proposal is to avoid eviction for such ast error (caused by failover umount) by checking error code (-ENODEV) in ldlm_handle_as_error().

If an eviction happened before failover umount, I think the tgt_client_del() should delete the client slot, however, if we change it to checking obd_fail (like what your patch did), it'll incorrectly leave the slot when failover umount set obd_fail later.

Patch Set 5:

The rootcase is that class_disconnect_export_list was already started with outdated flags when failover happens.

It doesn't matter if this client wasn't evicted because new target will handle it and the client may become online at that moment. 

You may propose an another fix but this patch works  already. Do you see any defects ?

Patch Set 5:

Let me try to make it clear, could you answer my three questions one by one? Thanks.

1. Server may fail to send CP/BL ast when failover umount started, and that can lead to eviction mistakenly. We all agree that this is a defect, and my proposed fix is to avoid eviction when ast failed for failover umount (by checking return value). Is my proposal viable?

2. The defect mentioned in 1. has nothing to do with the function class_disconnect_export_list() (I don't see it's called on the code path). Do you agree with me on this?

3. I think if class_disconnect_export_list() is called before failover umount started, then it should keep using the old export flags no matter if failover umount happened later, because this export disconnect operation was caused by a real eviction but not failover umount, and the on disk client record should be erased upon this real eviction. However, your patch oppositely skip such disk update in such situation, isn't this a bug?

Patch Set 5:

> 3. I think if class_disconnect_export_list() is called before failover umount started, then it should keep using the old export flags no matter if failover umount happened later, because this export disconnect operation was caused by a real eviction but not failover umount, and the on disk client record should be erased upon this real eviction. However, your patch oppositely skip such disk update in such situation, isn't this a bug?

replacement of the export flag check by checking of obd->obd_fail does help. Customer doesn't see evictions anymore. I do not understand why the code should maintain an uptodate copy of obd_fail 
 flag in export object if it is easier and more reliable to check obd->obd_fail?  The exact scenario why OBD_OPT_FAILOVER flag is not uptodate is not clear for us. What is known that the mechanism is complex and prone to bugs.

Patch Set 5:

Mike, could you review it? The export flag was mainly used by recovery thread, is it possible to get rid of it? My concern is that patch could make the real eviction (not caused by failover umount) being ignored during failover umount.

Patch Set 5:

I don't see how patch solves the problem, if there is the race then it exists still, e.g. class_disconnect_export_list() is called before obd_fail was set to 1, so there is a window before it will become 1 in which client slot can be still deleted. And that is the thing I don't like about this patch, the class_disconnect_export_list() becomes tricky, it can now delete some client slots and keep others and nobody can predict how it will be done. Just consider situation when it is started to evict clients in the list and obd_fail is set in the middle of that process.

Also I agree with Niu that patch opens new issues with real evictions which can be skipped. I am not sure if this is so serious though, if some slots are kept wrongly than they will be deleted during recovery, this will only cause long recovery due to missing clients.

I think we should consider just one particular case when wrong evictions are happened and fix it in the way when solution will be clear and complete.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 7:

I think the problem described in this ticket has been fixed by https://review.whamcloud.com/23921 , and I didn't see how Mike's concern about this solution is addressed by last version.

Patch Set 7:

well, my concerns weren't handled so I still think it introduces just new tricky bug

Patch Set 7:

Mike,sorry, I did not notice that.
Do you think the new problem introduced is worse than the original one (ie is it worth reverting this patch for now?)

Patch Set 7:

Oleg, the original problem is supposed to be fixed with other patch - 23921, thus if it is so, this patch just introduced new problem without fixing anything.

parents.subject: LU-9305 osd-zfs: arc_buf could be non-pagesize aligned

commit.subject: LU-8359 ldlm: Wrong evict during failover
commit.message:
LU-8359 ldlm: Wrong evict during failover

There is a race between setting obd_fail & OBD_OPT_FAILOVER.
tgt_client_del() checks only OBD_OPT_FAILOVER,
class_disconnect_export_list() is called with flags copied
from obd, and umount can start while disconnect
is in progress.

It is better to rely only on obd_fail.
We shouldn't evict during failover at all, it should
be handled on a new server.
Such wrong evict can happen when server can't send CP AST
to the client because failover has started already.

Change-Id: I649d35d180b2239fe558b375872d3805629968a9
Seagate-bug-id: MRP-3604
Signed-off-by: Andriy Skulysh <andriy.skulysh@seagate.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/21114
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Ben Evans <bevans@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexander Zarochentsev <alexander.zarochentsev@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
